steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Points drop $20\%$ every $5$ seconds, starting at $490$. A $20\%$ decrease means each interval keeps $80\%$, so each $5$-second interval multiplies the score by $0.80$. Since $x$ seconds contains $\frac{x}{5}$ of those intervals, the model is $$490(0.80)^{x/5}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "$f(x)=490(0.80)^{\frac{x}{5}}$": decay factor 0.80 per 5-second interval, exponent $x/5$ counts intervals. ✓
  • "$f(x)=490(0.80)^{5x}$": exponent $5x$ multiplies intervals by 5 instead of dividing — way too fast a decay. ✗
  • "$f(x)=490(0.20)^{\frac{x}{5}}$": 0.20 is the amount lost, not the amount kept — this drops to nearly zero far too fast. ✗
  • "$f(x)=490(0.20)^{5x}$": wrong factor and wrong exponent. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
